data.title: Add consumer_completeness dimension to Scope reviewer and update scrutiny pipeline
data.description:
## Testing Mode
UPDATE

## TDD Requirement
Task 53a0-e084 creates RED tests that fail before this task. After this task:
- test_scope_reviewer_has_consumer_completeness_dimension passes GREEN
- test_pipeline_scope_passes_part_c_output passes GREEN

## Implementation Steps
1. Edit plugins/dso/skills/shared/docs/reviewers/scope.md:
   a. Add 4th row to ## Your Dimensions table:
      | consumer_completeness | All consumers of artifacts created/modified by this epic (discovered by Part C scan) are covered by success criteria; any uncovered consumer is either explicitly descoped or addressed by the epic | Part C scan reveals consumers not covered by any success criterion and no descoping rationale is provided; score below 4 blocks the epic |
   b. Add 'consumer_completeness' to the JSON schema dimensions block
   c. Add N/A scoring note: score N/A when Part C scan was skipped (no consumers found)

2. Edit plugins/dso/skills/shared/workflows/epic-scrutiny-pipeline.md:
   a. Line 196: extend to mention passing Part C output for Scope reviewer
   b. Line 202 table row for Scope reviewer: add consumer_completeness to Dimensions column

## File Impact
- plugins/dso/skills/shared/docs/reviewers/scope.md: modify (add dimension)
- plugins/dso/skills/shared/workflows/epic-scrutiny-pipeline.md: modify (update Step 4)

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] tests/run-all.sh passes (exit 0)
  Verify: bash $(git rev-parse --show-toplevel)/tests/run-all.sh
- [ ] consumer_completeness dimension exists in scope.md
  Verify: grep -q 'consumer_completeness' $(git rev-parse --show-toplevel)/plugins/dso/skills/shared/docs/reviewers/scope.md
- [ ] consumer_completeness appears in pipeline Step 4 table
  Verify: grep -q 'consumer_completeness' $(git rev-parse --show-toplevel)/plugins/dso/skills/shared/workflows/epic-scrutiny-pipeline.md
- [ ] structural tests GREEN
  Verify: bash $(git rev-parse --show-toplevel)/tests/skills/test-scope-reviewer-consumer-completeness.sh
